title: SieveErrorKind missing Display impl
description: SieveErrorKind is a public enum used for programmatic error categorisation. It has Debug but no Display. The standard Rust error handling convention (enforced by thiserror and expected by tracing/log consumers) is that all user-visible error types implement Display. Without Display, callers who want to include the kind in a log message or a higher-level error string must fall back to {:?}. Idiomatic fix: impl fmt::Display for SieveErrorKind with concise human-readable variant descriptions, e.g. UnsupportedExtension(s) => write!(f, "unsupported extension: {s}"). Because SieveError already delegates its Display to self.message (which is a String), SieveErrorKind::Display would be additive and does not conflict.
